assumptions: No incident.io account, organization, connected integration, installed Slack app, main incidents channel, or declared incident is assumed.
The candidate assumes a user in a company Slack workspace with a corporate email address and access to a Slack Workspace Admin for installation.
Slack either permits public-channel creation or an administrator can use one of incident.io's documented channel-permission recovery paths.
No signup, authorization, app installation, channel creation, or incident declaration was performed during research.

developer_goal: Create an incident.io account through Slack, install the incident.io app, declare a first live incident, and observe the dedicated incident channel and main-channel announcement.

first_success.outcome: A first live incident has been declared and incident.io has created its dedicated Slack incident channel and posted the default heads-up in the main incidents channel.
first_success.observable_signal: The dedicated incident Slack channel opens or appears, and a heads-up appears in the main incidents channel.

route_selection.surface: Slack signup, app installation, and first live incident
route_selection.rule: Select the lexicographically shortest complete official route to the same first-success outcome after fixing product intent; if peer routes remain consequential, mark needs-human-judgment.

candidates.name: Slack signup, app installation, and first live incident
candidates.reason: Recorded as a reconstructable candidate, but public evidence does not close signup-field opacity and/or peer-route lexicographic selection for a verified count.

candidates.name: Microsoft Teams getting started
candidates.reason: The help center presents Microsoft Teams as a peer chat surface with a different installation and interaction path.

candidates.name: Tutorial incident
candidates.reason: The five-minute `/inc tutorial` guide is designed for practice rather than producing the same first real-incident terminal state.

candidates.name: Test incident
candidates.reason: A test incident avoids announcement and reporting side effects, but it is not posted in the main incidents channel or incident library and therefore changes the observable completion signal.

required_path.action: Complete incident.io account creation or trial signup at the official entry URL.

required_fields.label: Signup identity and required account fields
required_fields.notes: Public evidence does not establish the complete current required field set behind the client-rendered or authenticated signup surface.

required_path.observable_result: A new incident.io account or trial session exists.

required_path.action: Complete any required email or identity verification for the new account.
required_path.observable_result: The account becomes usable for developer onboarding.

required_path.action: Complete the documented post-login setup for the selected incident.io candidate through first success.

required_fields.label: Candidate-specific configuration
required_fields.notes: Exact fields depend on unresolved product, auth, or framework selection.

required_path.observable_result: The dedicated incident Slack channel opens or appears, and a heads-up appears in the main incidents channel.

prerequisites.description: {'order': 1, 'type': 'identity', 'requirement': 'Use Sign in with Slack from a company workspace with a corporate email address; personal email signup is restricted.', 'required': True, 'source_ids': ['S2']}
{'order': 2, 'type': 'permission', 'requirement': 'A Slack administrator is required to grant the app permissions used by incident.io.', 'required': True, 'source_ids': ['S2']}

{'order': 3, 'type': 'configuration', 'requirement': 'The workspace must permit incident.io to create the main incidents channel and per-incident public channels, either through general channel-creation permission or the documented admin-user option.', 'required': True, 'source_ids': ['S2']}

external_gates.description: Slack and Microsoft Teams are peer getting-started surfaces with different installation and interaction paths.
Slack signup is restricted to corporate or company email addresses.
A Slack administrator must authorize the app because of Slack's permission model.

unavoidable_waits.description: Email or identity-provider verification delivery when the selected account route requires it.

excluded.reason: Listed as excluded after success in the source record.
Listed as excluded after success in the source record.

evidence_supported: Primary documented developer route and first-success framing

uncertainties.question: Should the representative incident.io first mile use Slack or Microsoft Teams?
uncertainties.impact: The choice changes identity, admin authorization, installation, interaction, and terminal communication surfaces.
uncertainties.evidence_needed: Resolve against current official docs and a dated fresh-account or cold walkthrough: The official help center presents both as peer getting-started routes and does not designate one universal default.

uncertainties.question: Should first success be a live, test, tutorial, or retrospective incident?
uncertainties.impact: The modes intentionally differ in announcements, incident-library visibility, Insights inclusion, and response side effects.
uncertainties.evidence_needed: Resolve against current official docs and a dated fresh-account or cold walkthrough: The incident-mode documentation explains each mode but leaves the safety and visibility choice to the organization or user.

uncertainties.question: Which declaration interface should represent the general path?
uncertainties.impact: Slash command, Slack message, and browser entry require different interactions and inputs before the same or similar incident outcome.
uncertainties.evidence_needed: Resolve against current official docs and a dated fresh-account or cold walkthrough: The declaration guide documents these as alternatives without selecting one for all users.

uncertainties.question: What exact current required signup, identity, legal, and entitlement fields does incident.io show for a new developer, and which peer route is the lexicographic shortest path?
uncertainties.impact: Without those fields and a closed route choice, action and field counts cannot be verified for peer comparison.
uncertainties.evidence_needed: A dated read-only walkthrough of the live signup and the selected post-login first-success path, with screenshots of required fields and confirmation that peer routes are longer or gated.
